ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-03-2021 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kerry Riordan Sykes, Esq., counsel for William E. Sorensen, Jr., Petitioner (appearing by video conferencing).

At the Court's inquiry, Ms. Riordan Sykes confirms that no opposition or objection has been received to the Petition now before the Court. The Court acknowledges review of the various pleadings filed and, as the matter regards the admission of a lost will, finds that there is sufficient evidence to support rebuttal of Probate Code Sec. 6124 presumptions.

The Court admits into probate the copy of the lost will dated September 2, 1998 and the original codicil dated August 4, 2018. Shirley Yawitz is appointed probate referee.
The Court otherwise takes the matter under submission pending review of the Order.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kerry Sykes appeared by telephone for William Sorensen, Jr. (Petitioner).

The Court referred to Probate Code 6124 and noted that there is no duplicate original of the will. Ms. Sykes stated that they don't have an original of the will or the first codicil, and asked for a continuance so that she can file a declaration rebutting presumption of revocation. Ms. Sykes also stated that the Schneebelis had been Clients since 1998, that when the Husband died in 2017 Margaret (Wife) relied very heavily on her for advice and that if Margaret had changed her will, Margaret would've come to her. In addition, Ms. Sykes stated that Margaret had capacity in 2018, but was not very mobile, that the Son sold the house quickly, and that she believes the documents (will and codicil) were in the house and disposed of. Furthermore, Ms. Sykes stated that the subject of this case are bonds that are valued at $300,000.00, that there are also bonds in the trust which are of higher value and that she does not know why these bands are not in the trust as they should be. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Sykes is to file her declaration by August 17, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguers. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
